# Number Field

Edits a number with localized formatting and increment and decrement controls.

## Installation

Add Number Field from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/number-field
```

## Usage

Use Number Field when users may type a number or adjust it in known increments. Bind `v-model` to a number and place the input plus optional stepper buttons inside `NumberFieldContent`.

**Quantity example**

```vue
<template>
  <NumberField
    id="quantity"
    v-model="value"
    :min="0"
    :max="100"
    class="w-40"
  >
    <Label for="quantity">Quantity</Label>
    <NumberFieldContent>
      <NumberFieldDecrement />
      <NumberFieldInput />
      <NumberFieldIncrement />
    </NumberFieldContent>
  </NumberField>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import {
  NumberField,
  NumberFieldContent,
  NumberFieldDecrement,
  NumberFieldIncrement,
  NumberFieldInput,
} from '@/Components/NumberField'

const value = ref(12)
</script>
```

## Bounds and steps

Set `min`, `max`, and `step` on the root. Increment, decrement, typed input, and keyboard changes are clamped to the bounds. Step snapping is enabled by default; set `step-snapping="false"` only when off-grid values are valid. The model remains a number regardless of its displayed formatting.

## Decimal, percentage, and currency formatting

Pass `locale` and standard `Intl.NumberFormatOptions` through `format-options`. Formatting also determines which typed characters can be parsed. A percent formatter normally stores the fractional numeric value, while currency formatting stores only the amount; currency codes and units belong in separate domain state when they can vary.

```vue
<NumberField
  v-model="price"
  locale="en-US"
  :min="0"
  :step="0.01"
  :format-options="{ style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' }"
>
  <!-- NumberFieldContent and controls -->
</NumberField>
```

## States and wheel input

Use `disabled` when the field cannot be changed and `readonly` when its value should remain focusable. Mouse-wheel stepping applies only while the input is focused; set `disable-wheel-change` where scrolling the page could cause costly accidental changes. The stepper buttons disable automatically when another step cannot move within the configured bounds.

## Keyboard and accessibility

Give the root an `id` and associate it with a visible label. The input exposes spinbutton semantics. Arrow Up and Arrow Down move by one step, Page Up and Page Down by ten steps, and Home and End move to configured minimum and maximum values. Keep a textual unit in the label or description when formatting alone may be ambiguous.

## Forms and validation

Set `name` and `required` on the root for form participation. Validate the numeric model rather than the localized display text. Put `aria-invalid` on `NumberFieldInput` and render a nearby error for domain rules that go beyond minimum, maximum, and step. Avoid silently coercing an empty required field to zero.

## Choosing an input

> **Use Slider for approximate adjustment: [Slider](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/slider)**
>
> Use Slider when relative position and quick adjustment matter more than typing or reading an exact value. Keep Number Field for precise entry.

> **Use Input for numeric identifiers: [Input](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/input)**
>
> Phone numbers, postal codes, card fragments, and other digit strings are identifiers rather than quantities; keep them as text in Input.

Hosted documentation: [Number Field](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/number-field)
